skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Guides users to split independent debugging or test failures into focused parallel agent tasks, then review and integrate the results.
skill.summary: Dispatch focused agents for independent failures and integrate their fixes safely.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged seven external command patterns and three blocker patterns, but all were false positives after review of SKILL.md. The flagged locations are Markdown diagrams, prompt examples, and normal verification guidance, with no executable scripts, weak cryptography, reconnaissance behavior, prompt injection, or data exfiltration found.

content.value_statement: Multiple unrelated failures can waste time when one agent investigates them sequentially. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code split independent work into focused parallel agent tasks.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when failures are independent enough for parallel investigation.
Shows how to group failures by test file, subsystem, or problem domain.
Provides a focused prompt structure for assigning one agent per problem.
Explains how to review returned summaries before integrating changes.
Recommends full-suite verification after parallel fixes are combined.
limitations: It does not execute agents by itself or provide automation scripts.
It is not suitable when failures share state or likely have one root cause.
It assumes the user can review agent output and resolve conflicts.
It focuses on debugging workflows, not general project planning.

use_cases.title: Split Test Failure Triage
use_cases.description: Assign separate agents to unrelated failing test files, then compare their root cause summaries.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Refactor Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Use parallel agents to investigate independent regressions after a large code refactor.

use_cases.title: Speed Up Bug Investigation
use_cases.description: Turn several unrelated bugs into scoped agent tasks with clear constraints and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Check If Parallel Agents Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: I have these failures: [list failures]. Decide whether they are independent enough for parallel agents. Group them by domain and explain any shared-state risks.

prompt_templates.title: Create Focused Agent Tasks
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare clear assignments for multiple agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these independent failures into focused agent prompts. Each prompt must include scope, goal, constraints, and expected output: [paste failures].

prompt_templates.title: Review Parallel Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Integrate returned findings from several agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these agent summaries for conflicts, duplicated changes, and missing verification steps. Recommend the safest integration order: [paste summaries].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Full Debugging Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for a complex regression set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a parallel debugging plan for these failures. Include domain grouping, agent prompts, conflict checks, verification commands, and final integration criteria: [paste context].

output_examples.input: Three unrelated test files fail after a refactor.
output_examples.output: The skill would group the failures by file, assign one agent to each file, and require each agent to return root cause and change summaries.

output_examples.input: Two failures may share a data model bug.
output_examples.output: The skill would advise investigating them together first because a shared root cause could make parallel fixes conflict.

output_examples.input: Agents returned three fixes with no overlapping files.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend reviewing summaries, checking for hidden behavior conflicts, running the full suite, and then integrating the fixes.

best_practices: Use parallel agents only when each task has a narrow and independent scope.
Give each agent the relevant errors, constraints, and expected summary format.
Review every result and run full verification before accepting combined changes.
anti_patterns: Do not dispatch agents when one root cause may explain all failures.
Do not give broad prompts such as fixing all tests without boundaries.
Do not integrate parallel changes without checking conflicts and rerunning tests.

faq.question: Does this skill run multiple agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompt structure for tools that support parallel agent tasks.

faq.question: When should I avoid this workflow?
faq.answer: Avoid it when failures are related, share state, or require one complete system-level investigation.

faq.question: Can it help with non-test bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help with separate bugs across independent subsystems when each task has clear boundaries.

faq.question: How many agents should I dispatch?
faq.answer: Use one agent per independent problem domain. The skill is written for three or more independent failures.

faq.question: What should each agent return?
faq.answer: Each agent should return the root cause, the changes made or recommended, and any verification performed.

faq.question: Is this safe for shared code areas?
faq.answer: Use caution. If agents may edit the same files or shared logic, investigate sequentially or add strict constraints.
